readyToStart.title: CRM-to-portal delivery & billing sync (Leangency 14-day flow). On deal-won, provision the portal tenant + push initial delivery state, then mirror billing as it changes. Adds SetupInstalment model + DeliveryTier enum + tenantSlug on Opportunity; Stripe invoice create/finalize for the 3x GBP167 Setup fee (D0/7/14); StripeSubscription persistence on customer.subscription.* webhooks; a PortalDeliverySync service that POSTs /api/internal/tenants then /api/internal/delivery via the domain_events outbox; extends CloseDealWonUseCase; registers a deal_won_enriched handler in events-worker; adds a daily portal-delivery-sweep BullMQ job in ai-worker; and verifies the Stripe webhook signature (handleSubscriptionWebhook moved off tRPC).
readyToStart.description: CRM-to-portal delivery & billing sync (Leangency 14-day flow). On deal-won, provision the portal tenant + push initial delivery state, then mirror billing as it changes. readyToStart.summary: IFC-314 — N+1 Query-Budget Detector + cross-context N+1 remediation, governed by ADR-053 (Accepted). DETECTOR (packages/db/src/query-budget/): AsyncLocalStorage request-scoped store (context.ts:... All 56 findings fixed-or-documented;...
readyToStart.definitionOfDone: SetupInstalment + DeliveryTier + tenantSlug on Opportunity (migration applied); Stripe adapter createInvoiceItem/createInvoice/finalizeInvoice; StripeSubscription persisted on customer.subscription.*; PortalDeliverySync POSTs provision + delivery via the domain_events outbox; CloseDealWonUseCase creates 3 instalments + finalises instalment-1 + enqueues the push; events-worker handles opportunity.deal_won_enriched; ai-worker runs a daily portal-delivery-sweep job; handleSubscriptionWebhook verifies the Stripe signature; artifacts: packages/db, packages/adapters, packages/application, apps/api, apps/workers/events-worker, apps/ai-worker; targets: >=90% scoped coverage, idempotent push; verified by: pnpm typecheck/test/lint/build + pre-ship

readyToStart.title: Real inbox integration - contact.draftReply real-thread resolver. IFC-312 audit fix F5 follow-up: the draftReply procedure currently requires the client to supply a full emailThread array (server returns PRECONDITION_FAILED otherwise). IFC-313 replaces this with a server-side resolver that takes input.emailThreadId and fetches the actual ContactEmailThread history (pending real inbox ingestion infra - to be scoped separately). Keep the client-supplied emailThread path as a fallback for power users / integrations.

tasks.title: Lead Router Security - Tenant Isolation & Domain Bypass. Fix 4 CRITICAL/MEDIUM security issues: (1) scoreWithAI fire-and-forget uses raw ctx.prisma instead of prismaWithTenant - cross-tenant data leak risk [S2]. (2) logActivity $transaction uses raw ctx.prisma - may bypass tenant middleware [S3]. (3) bulkUpdateStatus uses direct Prisma updateMany bypassing Lead.changeStatus() domain validation - skips status transition rules and domain events [S4]. (4) getHotLeads/getReadyForQualification pass userId as ownerId without explicit tenantId [S9]. Fix: Replace ctx.prisma with prismaWithTenant in all cases. Route bulkUpdateStatus through domain model. Add explicit tenantId to hot/ready queries.
tasks.description: Lead Router Security - Tenant Isolation & Domain Bypass. (2) logActivity $transaction uses raw ctx.prisma - may bypass tenant middleware [S3]. (3) bulkUpdateStatus uses direct Prisma updateMany bypassing Lead.changeStatus() domain validation - skips status transition rules and domain events [S4]. (4) getHotLeads/getReadyForQualification pass userId as ownerId without explicit tenantId [S9]. Fix: Replace ctx.prisma with prismaWithTenant in all cases. Route bulkUpdateStatus through domain model. Add explicit tenantId to hot/ready queries.

tasks.title: Lead Router Data Integrity Fixes. Fix 5 HIGH issues: (1) update procedure ambiguous status field handling - may silently drop or bypass domain validation [S5]. (2) Score tier thresholds inconsistent - domain LeadScore uses 80/50 but router stats query uses 70/40 [S6]. (3) sortBy accepts arbitrary strings with no enum validation - Prisma orderBy injection risk [S7]. (4) leadListResponseSchema expects 'data' key but router returns 'leads' [W4]. (5) leadResponseSchema missing Lead 360 fields (aiInsight, activities, notes, files, tasks) [D5]. Fix: Explicitly handle status in update, unify thresholds, add sortBy enum, fix schema keys.

tasks.title: Contact Router Security — Tenant Isolation Fixes. CRITICAL: (1) search procedure has no createTenantWhereClause — cross-tenant search by name/email (R-02). (2) logActivity transaction uses raw ctx.prisma with no tenantId in WHERE — cross-tenant update (R-03). (3) bulkEmail/bulkExport no tenant filter — email exfiltration via guessed UUIDs (R-04). (4) stats ignores typedCtx tenant — no tenantId boundary (R-05). (5) getById/getByEmail double-fetch through unscoped domain repo (R-06). Cross-ref: R-01 (prismaWithTenant raw) is cross-cutting and shared with IFC-237. Audit: docs/audit/contact-detail-wiring-audit.md §15.

tasks.title: Account Backend Tenant Isolation & Security. (1) CRITICAL: filterOptions uses raw ctx.prisma instead of prismaWithTenant for user lookup — tenant data leak (B-01, account.router.ts:445). (2) PrismaAccountRepository has NO tenantId filter on any method — single-layer defense (B-02). (3) existsByName blocks same name across all tenants — should be per-tenant (B-03). (4) sortBy parameter unsanitized — potential Prisma orderBy injection (B-04). (5) TOCTOU window in update/delete without transaction (B-05). (6) Zero audit logging on all account mutations (B-07). Audit: docs/audit/account-detail-wiring-audit.md sections 11-12.

tasks.title: Deal Detail Page — Replace SAMPLE_DEAL with Real API Data. CRITICAL: Entire deal detail page (deals/[id]/page.tsx 792 lines) is 100% hardcoded with SAMPLE_DEAL constant (lines 116-150) — zero API calls. The dealId from useParams() is captured but never used for data fetch (line 684). Must: (1) Replace SAMPLE_DEAL with trpc.opportunity.getById query using dealId param. (2) Add useRequireAuth() guard (F-03 — detail page is the only unguarded page). (3) Fix STAGES constant to match domain 7-stage enum (F-04 — uses CLOSED instead of CLOSED_WON/CLOSED_LOST, omits NEEDS_ANALYSIS). (4) Fix StageProgress to use deal prop instead of SAMPLE_DEAL.value directly (F-05). (5) Remove _SAMPLE_ACTIVITIES dead code (F-06). (6) Add clickable links to account/contact detail pages (F-07). (7) Wire real owner data from getById relation include. Router getById exists (IFC-186). Cross-entity patterns from Lead 360 wiring (2026-03-04) should be followed. Audit: docs/audit/deal-detail-wiring-audit.md section 1.

tasks.title: Deal Router Correctness Fixes. (1) Name updates silently dropped — service has TODO 'Add updateName method' (B-04, OpportunityService.ts:313). Add updateName to domain and wire in service. (2) data.probability?.value unwrapping may be undefined if percentageSchema returns plain number (B-05, router:460). Verify and fix. (3) opportunityListResponseSchema key 'data' vs router key 'opportunities' mismatch (B-07, validators:88 vs router:437). (4) throwOpportunityCreateError maps VALIDATION_ERROR to NOT_FOUND — wrong HTTP semantic (B-09, router:55). (5) validateStageDeactivation throws plain Error → 500 instead of BAD_REQUEST (B-10, validators:214). (6) mapOpportunityToResponse drops closedAt field (B-11, mappers.ts:88-108). (7) CLOSED_LOST no-reason passes Zod but fails at service (B-12, router:322). Audit: docs/audit/deal-detail-wiring-audit.md section 6.

tasks.title: Deal Filter Wiring & Type Safety. (1) DealFilters state (filters) captured in page but NEVER passed to trpc.opportunity.list.useQuery — ownerId and dateRange selections have no effect on kanban view data (F-10, deals/(list)/page.tsx:214,226). (2) 'More Filters' button has no onClick handler (F-11, DealFilters.tsx). (3) Owner filter only has 2 hardcoded options: 'All Deals' and 'My Deals' — no dynamic user list (F-12). (4) transformDeals uses loose Record<string,unknown> casting at API boundary instead of typed response (F-19, types.ts:102-119). Wire filters to API query params; add server-side owner filter with user list; type-safe API response handling. Audit: docs/audit/deal-detail-wiring-audit.md sections 3, 8.

tasks.title: IFC-311 reassign UI wiring follow-up (contacts + accounts) — wire the four new tRPC procedures shipped by IFC-311 (api.contact.reassign, api.contact.bulkReassign, api.account.reassign, api.account.bulkReassign) into real UI so the notifyOnOwnerChange Cat-1 toggle is reachable for end users on both entities. Source: IFC-311 post-completion code review Finding 1 — all four procedures are dead-on-arrival at runtime (zero direct UI callers). The account SINGLE-reassign path is transitively reachable via AccountDetail.tsx:152 → api.account.assignOwner (refactored in IFC-311 to delegate to performAccountReassign, so the notification fires), but api.account.bulkReassign is fully orphaned and the account detail page still calls the legacy name. Scope: (a) contacts-list bulk-actions bar exposes a Reassign action that calls contact.bulkReassign; (b) contact detail page actions expose a Reassign option that calls contact.reassign; (c) accounts-list bulk-actions bar exposes a Reassign action that calls account.bulkReassign (brand-new UI surface — no legacy shim covers this); (d) migrate AccountDetail.tsx:152 from api.account.assignOwner.useMutation to api.account.reassign.useMutation so the canonical IFC-311 name is the production caller (assignOwner stays as a deprecated legacy alias); (e) one integration test per entity that exercises the toggle flipping the notification path end-to-end. Backend API surface is already shipped by IFC-311 — this task is UI + wiring + one cosmetic endpoint migration only.
tasks.description: IFC-311 reassign UI wiring follow-up (contacts + accounts) — wire the four new tRPC procedures shipped by IFC-311 (api.contact.reassign, api.contact.bulkReassign, api.account.reassign, api.account.bulkReassign) into real UI so the notifyOnOwnerChange Cat-1 toggle is reachable for end users on both entities. Source: IFC-311 post-completion code review Finding 1 — all four procedures are dead-on-arrival at runtime (zero direct UI callers). The account SINGLE-reassign path is transitively reachable via AccountDetail.tsx:152 → api.account.assignOwner (refactored in IFC-311 to delegate to performAccountReassign, so the notification fires), but api.account.bulkReassign is fully orphaned and the account detail page still calls the legacy name. Scope: (a) contacts-list bulk-actions bar exposes a Reassign action that calls contact.bulkReassign; (b) contact detail page actions expose a Reassign option that calls contact.reassign; (c) accounts-list bulk-actions bar exposes a Reassign action that calls account.bulkReassign (brand-new UI surface — no legacy shim covers this); (d) migrate AccountDetail.tsx:152 from api.account.assignOwner.useMutation to api.account.reassign.useMutation so the canonical IFC-311 name is the production caller (assignOwner stays as a deprecated legacy alias); (e) one integration test per entity that exercises the toggle flipping the notification path end-to-end. Backend API surface is already shipped by IFC-311 — this task is UI + wiring + one cosmetic endpoint migration only.

tasks.title: Real inbox integration - contact.draftReply real-thread resolver. IFC-312 audit fix F5 follow-up: the draftReply procedure currently requires the client to supply a full emailThread array (server returns PRECONDITION_FAILED otherwise). IFC-313 replaces this with a server-side resolver that takes input.emailThreadId and fetches the actual ContactEmailThread history (pending real inbox ingestion infra - to be scoped separately). Keep the client-supplied emailThread path as a fallback for power users / integrations.

tasks.title: Contact Domain Model Fixes. (1) reconstitute() silently substitutes unknown@unknown.com for invalid emails — should Result.fail or throw (D-01). (2) mergeContacts no cross-tenant check — linkToLead correctly checks but merge doesn't (D-02). (3) No ContactDeletedEvent — deleteContact publishes no events, audit handler has mapping but event never emitted (D-04). (4) No ContactEmailChangedEvent — old email not captured in generic ContactUpdatedEvent (D-05). (5) Tags update always emits event without deep equality check (D-07). Audit: docs/audit/contact-detail-wiring-audit.md §18.

tasks.title: Deal Forecast Algorithm Fixes. (1) buildWinRateTrend hardcodes months ['May','Jun','Jul','Aug','Sep','Oct'] regardless of current date — semantically incorrect data (B-06, forecast-algorithm.ts:283). Fix to derive from actual 6-month window. (2) DEFAULT_STAGE_PROBABILITIES diverge: validators has NEEDS_ANALYSIS=30/PROPOSAL=70 while domain/service/router/forecast-algorithm uses 40/60 (B-08). Unify to single source of truth. (3) buildMonthlyRevenue uses locale-dependent toLocaleString for month names — breaks on non-en-US servers (B-15, forecast-algorithm.ts:269). (4) Forecast trend values hardcoded as 12 and 5 in forecast page (F-16, deals/forecast/page.tsx:709-710). Compute from API data. Audit: docs/audit/deal-detail-wiring-audit.md sections 4, 6.

Unify to single source of truth. (3) buildMonthlyRevenue uses locale-dependent toLocaleString for month names — breaks on non-en-US servers (B-15, forecast-algorithm.ts:269). tasks.title: Lead Event Pipeline - Persistent Outbox + Webhooks. Fix HIGH/LOW issues: (1) InMemoryEventBus loses all events on server restart - no persistent outbox pattern [W3]. (2) No webhook handlers for lead events - external systems cannot subscribe [W9]. Fix: Implement transactional outbox pattern for lead domain events (LeadCreatedEvent, LeadQualifiedEvent, etc.). Add webhook dispatch for lead events. Ensure event ordering and at-least-once delivery.

Add webhook dispatch for lead events. Ensure event ordering and at-least-once delivery.

tasks.title: Contact Event Pipeline — Worker Handlers + Event Types. (1) Zero contact event handlers in events worker (W-02). (2) Only 2/8 contact event types in DOMAIN_EVENT_TYPES — missing deleted, account_associated, account_disassociated, converted_from_lead, linked_to_lead, interacted (W-04). (3) No webhook routing for contact events (W-05). (4) No notifications for contact events (W-11). Cross-ref: W-01 (InMemoryEventBus) shared with IFC-250. Audit: docs/audit/contact-detail-wiring-audit.md §17.

tasks.title: Contact Merge & Duplicate Detection Wiring. (1) mergeContacts deletes secondary without re-parenting all child records — orphaned opportunities/tasks (W-03). (2) findPotentialDuplicates exists in service but no tRPC procedure exposes it (W-09). (3) mergeContacts exists but no router procedure wraps it (W-10). (4) mergeContacts no cross-tenant check between primary and secondary (W-12). (5) associateWithAccount inside mergeContacts causes double-save and potential double event emission (W-13). Audit: docs/audit/contact-detail-wiring-audit.md §17.
tasks.description: Contact Merge & Duplicate Detection Wiring. (1) mergeContacts deletes secondary without re-parenting all child records — orphaned opportunities/tasks (W-03). (4) mergeContacts no cross-tenant check between primary and secondary (W-12). (5) associateWithAccount inside mergeContacts causes double-save and potential double event emission (W-13). Audit: docs/audit/contact-detail-wiring-audit.md §17.

tasks.title: Contact Email & AI Integration. (1) No email-to-contact matching — inbound emails not linked to contacts by sender address (W-06). Audit: docs/audit/contact-detail-wiring-audit.md §17.

tasks.title: Account Event Pipeline — Worker Handlers + Event Types. (1) Zero account event handlers in events worker (W-01). (2) Zero account event types in DOMAIN_EVENT_TYPES map (W-02). (3) Audit handler has 3 account event mappings (Created/Updated/Deleted) but events never reach it because worker has no handlers and dispatcher has no types (W-03). Add event handlers for AccountCreated, AccountUpdated, AccountDeleted (requires IFC-271), AccountRevenueUpdated, AccountHierarchyUpdated, AccountIndustryCategorized to events worker. Register all types in DOMAIN_EVENT_TYPES. Cross-ref: W-01/W-02 pattern shared with Contact (IFC-261). Audit: docs/audit/account-detail-wiring-audit.md section 14.

tasks.title: Agent/Conversation Router Frontend Wiring. 17 dead endpoints (13 conversation.* + 4 agent.*) with zero frontend callers. Frontend uses autoResponse/aiReview/aiMonitoring routers that query the same DB tables directly bypassing the canonical conversation router. Fix: wire all endpoints to proper frontend consumers — chat panel, agent-approvals, AI Search, admin dashboard — and refactor aiMonitoring to delegate to conversation router instead of duplicating DB queries. Audit: artifacts/reports/dead-endpoints-audit.csv rows 4-18.

tasks.title: Deal Service Dead Code & Minor Fixes. (1) 11 dead service methods unreachable from router: listOpportunities, advanceStage, updateValue, updateProbability, updateExpectedCloseDate, reopenOpportunity, getPipelineForecast, getOpportunitiesClosingSoon, getHighValueOpportunities, getOpportunitiesByAccount, getOpportunitiesByContact, getWinRateStatistics (B-13). Decide: wire to router or remove. (2) toMoney/toPercentage silent zero fallback on DB corruption (B-14). (3) TOCTOU in stage transitions — no optimistic locking (B-16). (4) Event publish failure swallowed with console.error only (B-17). (5) DealQuickView is a 49-line stub not used anywhere (F-13). (6) RecommendedActions.onActionClick not wired at forecast page level (F-18). (7) Sidebar deals config settingsHref points to /settings/deals instead of /settings/pipeline (W-03). Audit: docs/audit/deal-detail-wiring-audit.md sections 6-7.
